enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request about reggae music and Rastafarianism, sharing rambling personal opinions and a jailhouse anecdote about a man who toured with Bob Marley. He covers the origins of reggae, his view that Rastafarianism has two faces (the religious/Ethiopia aspect and a stoner lifestyle religion), and claims personal identification as a Rasta.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: invented reggae and believed music could fix all the world's problems by spreading positivity
has two separate faces: the Haile Selassie/Ethiopia religious side and the stoner lifestyle side, similar to how Scientology has two faces
Rastafarianism is essentially the original stoner religion and provides a good cultural cohesive identity for that community
identifies himself as a Rasta and challenges anyone who disputes it
thinks it was a good thing
likes it, especially Bob Marley's Legend album from around 10th grade; hasn't been listening to it much lately
